semanticCore: Rushing headlong, charging, entering blindly, steep slope, or hurdle
primaryMeaning: Rushing headlong or scaling a steep slope
quranicRelevance: Scaling the steep hurdle (Iqtahama - 'fal-aqtahama al-'aqabata'), and armies charging headlong into Hell ('muqtahimun').

keyDerivations.meaning: To rush headlong, charge blindly, force entry, scale a steep slope, or scale hurdles

figurativeOrRareUsages: هَٰذَا فَوْجٌ مُقْتَحِمٌ مَعَكُمْ ۖ لَا مَرْحَبًا بِهِمْ (38:59): 'This is a crowd charging headlong with you (into Hell); no welcome for them!' (mutual curses of leaders/followers).
summaryEn: The root represents rushing headlong and scaling hurdles (Iqtiham), illustrating the command to scale the moral hurdle of charity, and the chaotic charging of sinners into Hell.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر الرمي بالنفس في الشدائد والدخول المفاجئ بعسر (قحم)، واصفاً اقتحام عقبة البذل المالي الصعب، وازدحام ودخول الكفار جهنم.
rawText: إِقْتَحَمَ 8 . See 1. === Said of a young camel: see voce بُلَعٌ. === اِقْتَحَمَ الغَمَراَتِ: see 1 in art. خوض. And اقتحم العَقَبَةَ: see عَقَبَةٌ. تَقَحَّمَ 5 He experienced dearth, drought, or sterility. See an ex. voce تَبِعَةٌ. === تَقَحَّمَ فِى الأَمْرِ بِلاَ رَوِيَّةٍ [He plunged, or rushed, into the affair without consideration]. (K, * TA in art. علط.) See 1. === تَقَحَّمَ: see تَدَلَّثَ. قحم نَفْسَهُ فِى الأُمُورِ 1 and 🡻تقحمّ and 🡻اقتحم He entered into affairs without consideration. (A.) قَحْمَةٌ , like قَحْبَةٌ, An old woman. See قَحْبٌ. مُقْحَمٌ Redundant; pleonastic; foisted in: applied to a word and to a letter. === حَرْفٌ مَقْحَمَةٌ A letter inserted without reason. مُقْحَمَةٌ is also applied in like manner to a word. [In a copy of the S, in art. بهت, I find it written مُقْحِمَةٌ]: i. q. زَائِدَةٌ. (TA in art. بهت.)
